title: Launch HN: Manufact (YC S25) – MCP Cloud
titleZh: 发布 HN：Manufact (YC S25) – MCP Cloud
author: pzullo
relevanceReason: 命中 5 个 AI/科技关键词。
textRawHtml:
Hi HN, we are Pietro and Luigi, cofounders of Manufact (https://manufact.com), a cloud for MCP apps and servers. We used to be called mcp-use, and still build open source SDKs for MCP under that name: https://github.com/mcp-use/mcp-use. We did a Show HN about that last year: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=44747229.

Today we want to tell you about our cloud product, Manufact, which is to mcp-use as Vercel is to Next.js. Manufact is an MCP vertical cloud designed for dev teams putting MCP Apps and servers in production.You can ship, iterate on, test and monitor your MCPs, and get them ready for the store submissions. All with the best developer and agent experience in mind.

Here is a demo video of the product: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R2rbr5OT9LI.

We have been working on MCP since April 2025. Our first focus was making it easy to build agents that could use any MCP server, and a lot of people started using our SDKs. Then the harness revolution kicked off: Claude Code, Claude Cowork, ChatGPT, Codex, OpenCode started shipping agent harnesses that made most standalone agent frameworks redundant. That pushed us to the other side of the connection, the servers. If agents were going to consolidate into a few harnesses, then first-class integration with the rest of a company's systems (i.e. MCP) would become the thing that mattered, so we started building up our server SDKs.

Then in succession:

1. Oct 2025. ChatGPT Apps SDK. OpenAI brings app UIs to ChatGPT, built on top of MCP and the work of mcp-ui. 2. Late 2025. The stores open. ChatGPT starts accepting app submissions, Claude grows its connector directory with selected partners. 3. Jan 2026. MCP Apps becomes official. SEP-1865 merges as the first MCP extension (io.modelcontextprotocol/ui): one UI standard any host can render.

Today, all the major clients fully support MCP and are opening marketplaces of reviewed MCPs that can be one click installed. All major tech companies have an MCP server, and many of those are reporting that already 15+% of their usage comes from their MCP, and we start to have a good way to distribute them just now.

MCP can return fully interactive UIs. So companies can (1) display data in more meaningful ways to their users (e.g. analytics, ecommerce) and (2) display their branding in some of the most used products on the planet (ChatGPT, Claude etc). Numbers: an engineer at Amplitude reported that their MCP saw a 2x increase in retention after adding UI to their MCP.

Clients (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor) are starting to dynamically present MCP servers/apps to users, based on their intent. Products will be organically discovered on the chats!

We feel that MCP is reaching its maturity moment. Now that MCPs are starting to be easy to install and discover, there is going to be a huge incentive for users to use them and for companies to create them:

1 - Most work is already done from AI chats, this is not going to stop, MCP gives you a way to interact with products without manually using their dashboards.

2 - MCP allows you to bring the context together in one place: you can read an email, create a ticket while plugged into the source code of your product, or your knowledge base. Aggregation of products that was not possible before, will happen in the chat, orchestrated by increasingly intelligent models.

If AI apps (Codex, Claude Desktop) are the new browsers, as PG said in a recent tweet https://x.com/paulg/status/2069080429236191504, then MCPs are the new websites.

But there is a catch:

- Submission process on the stores is still quite tricky, manual and takes up valuable time. - Hardly anybody knows how to design a good MCP: most of them are 1:1 proxies of the API and are abandoned, since being one shotted a few months ago. - The MCP Spec advances quickly and it is not easy to keep track of the changes, and what they mean for your server. - Auth is still a mystery for most teams (API key in the URL ???). - Most companies are not even aware that MCPs can return interactive UIs. - Clients still have to consolidate behavior, some do dynamic tool discovery, some don't, some persist authentication properly some don't.

We built Manufact and mcp-use to solve these problems. Our SDKs help them build good MCPs, our inspector helps them test locally, and our cloud helps them ship/publish and monitor them in production.

To deploy on Manufact you just need to connect a Github app, pick the repo, we'll detect the framework you are working with and get you a live MCP url as soon as possible.

In our platform, that live URL will be used to give you a chat where you can try/debug your MCP immediately and share it with your team. If you push an update on a new experimental branch, you'll be able to test that as well thanks to preview deployments.

Once your server is ready to go live, we help you make sure that it does not break. You can configure automated tests that will take your MCP server, install it in ChatGPT and Claude and test it. We do not test the model, we test the client (model + harness). This way you reliably know if your server breaks where people use it.

Since publishing on the store is a major distribution unlock for companies (your MCP can be dynamically discovered and one click installed across Claude products, and ChatGPT), we collected a set of requirements that will keep your submission from being rejected. You check this locally before going through the actual review process.

Once your server is live, you'll want to understand how it is used. Our analytics are designed for MCP, so you'll know how many users are hitting your MCP, how many tool calls you receive, from which client.

You can try out https://manufact.com for free today. We have usage-based pricing and on our free account we give free credits for you to try it out. If you have an MCP already, just connect your Github repo and deploy, if not you can build one using our skill and SDKs pretty simply (we will guide you in the onboarding).

We would love to hear feedback about the product in the comments, and hear thoughts from everyone about MCP. Thanks! :)
textZhHtml:
你好，HN，我们是Pietro和Luigi，Manufact的联合创始人（https://manufact.com），一个专为 MCP 应用和服务器打造的云平台。我们曾名为 mcp-use，至今仍以该名称开发 MCP 开源 SDK： https://github.com/mcp-use/mcp-use我们去年做了一期关于此事的节目HN： https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=44747229。

今天，我们想向大家介绍我们的云产品 Manufact，它之于 mcp-use，就如同 Vercel 之于 Next.js。Manufact 是一款专为将 MCP 应用和服务器投入生产环境的开发团队设计的 MCP 垂直云。您可以发布、迭代、测试和监控您的 MCP，并为应用商店提交做好准备。这一切都旨在打造最佳的开发者与智能体体验。

这是该产品的演示视频： https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R2rbr5OT9LI。

自2025年4月以来，我们一直致力于MCP的研发。我们最初的重点是降低开发门槛，让构建能够接入任意MCP服务器的智能体变得更加便捷，随后许多人开始使用我们的SDK。随后，智能体运行环境革命拉开帷幕：Claude Code、Claude Cowork、ChatGPT、Codex和OpenCode相继推出各自的智能体运行环境，使得大多数独立智能体框架变得不再必要。这促使我们将重心转向连接的另一端——服务端。既然智能体将逐渐整合到少数几个运行环境中，那么与企业其余系统（即MCP）的原生集成便成了重中之重，因此我们开始大力完善服务端SDK。

然后依次：

1. 2025年10月。ChatGPT 应用 SDK。OpenAI 将应用界面引入 ChatGPT，基于 MCP 和 mcp-ui 的成果构建。 2. 2025年底。应用商店开放。ChatGPT 开始接受应用提交，Claude 则与精选合作伙伴共同扩充其连接器目录。 3. 2026年1月。MCP 应用正式推出。SEP-1865 作为首个 MCP 扩展（io.modelcontextprotocol/ui）被合并：这是一项任何宿主都能渲染的统一 UI 标准。

如今，所有主要客户端均已全面支持 MCP，并正在开设经过审核的 MCP 市场，支持一键安装。各大科技公司都拥有自己的 MCP 服务器，其中许多公司报告称，其超过 15% 的使用量均来自 MCP，而我们如今也终于找到了分发它们的有效渠道。

MCP 可以返回完全交互式的用户界面。因此，企业能够（1）以更有意义的方式向用户展示数据（如数据分析、电子商务），并（2）在全球使用最广泛的产品（如 ChatGPT、Claude 等）中展示其品牌。数据方面：Amplitude 的一位工程师透露，在其 MCP 中添加 UI 后，用户留存率提升了 2 倍。

客户端（Claude、ChatGPT、Cursor）正开始根据用户意图，向用户动态展示 MCP 服务器/应用。产品将在对话中自然而然地被用户发现！

我们认为，MCP 正迎来其成熟期。随着 MCP 的安装与发现日益便捷，用户的使用意愿和企业的开发动力都将得到极大激发：

1 - 大部分工作已经可以通过AI对话来完成，这一趋势不会停止，MCP为您提供了一种无需手动操作其仪表盘即可与产品交互的方式。

2 - MCP 让您能够将上下文整合到一处：您可以阅读电子邮件、创建工单，同时接入您产品的源代码或知识库。过去无法实现的产品整合，将在对话中完成，并由日益智能的模型进行统筹调度。

如果人工智能应用程序（Codex、Claude Desktop）是新的浏览器，正如 PG 在最近的一条推文中所说 https://x.com/paulg/status/2069080429236191504，那么MCP就是新一代网站。

但有个陷阱：

- 应用商店的上架流程依然相当繁琐，高度依赖人工，且占用大量宝贵时间。 - 几乎没人知道如何设计一个优秀的 MCP：大多数仅仅是 API 的 1:1 代理，且早已被弃用，因为它们在几个月前就被迅速淘汰了。 - MCP 规范迭代迅速，很难及时跟进各项变更及其对服务器的具体影响。 - 鉴权机制对大多数团队而言仍是个谜（把 API 密钥放在 URL 里？？？）。 - 大多数公司甚至不知道 MCP 还能返回交互式 UI。 - 客户端仍需统一行为规范，有的支持动态工具发现，有的不支持；有的能正确持久化鉴权状态，有的则不能。

我们打造了 Manufact 和 mcp-use 来解决这些问题。 我们的 SDK 帮助他们构建优质的 MCP，我们的检查工具帮助他们在本地进行测试，我们的云服务则帮助他们将这些 MCP 部署/发布到生产环境并进行监控。

要在 Manufact 上部署，您只需连接 GitHub 应用并选择代码仓库，我们会自动检测您所使用的框架，并尽快为您提供一个实时 MCP URL。

在我们的平台上，该实时 URL 将为您提供一个对话界面，您可以立即试用或调试您的 MCP，并与团队共享。如果您在新的实验分支上推送更新，借助预览部署功能，您还可以直接对其进行测试。

您的服务器准备上线后，我们会协助您确保其不会出现故障。您可以配置自动化测试，该测试会获取您的 MCP 服务器，将其安装到 ChatGPT 和 Claude 中并进行测试。我们测试的不是模型本身，而是客户端（模型+测试框架）。这样一来，您就能确切地掌握您的服务器在用户实际使用场景中是否会出现故障。

由于在商店发布能为企业解锁重大分发渠道（您的 MCP 可被动态发现，并支持在 Claude 系列产品和 ChatGPT 中一键安装），我们整理了一套要求，以确保您的提交不被拒绝。您可以在进入正式审核流程前先在本地进行核查。

服务器上线后，您会希望了解其使用情况。我们的数据分析专为 MCP 设计，让您清楚有多少用户在访问您的 MCP，收到了多少次工具调用，以及这些调用来自哪些客户端。

您可以试用 https://manufact.com 今天即可免费使用。我们采用按量计费模式，免费账户会赠送免费额度供您体验。如果您已有 MCP，只需连接 GitHub 仓库并部署即可；若还没有，您也可以利用我们的技能和 SDK 轻松构建一个（我们会在新手引导中为您提供指导）。

我们非常期待在评论区看到大家对该产品的反馈，也欢迎大家分享对MCP的看法。谢谢！:)

Now I assume it is 🙂Paolo Perazzo@SiVolaGreat addition to the generic MCP Client collection.Kingsley Uyi Idehen@kidehenInfra isn't just plumbing. It's distribution. Examples: AgentMail: inboxes for agents, mcp-use: MCP infra, DeepAware: RL for data center ops. Whoever owns the rails agents use to talk to the outside world ends up with Twilio-style power.Vedika Jain@vedikaja_inmcp-use is by far the best Python framework for building an agent MCP. It's so easy to set up an agent connected to MCP servers through the command line, that way I can quickly iterate on my MCP servers and test. Most people are using MCP servers in chat clients like Cursor / Claude Code, but MCP server use by agents will explode in the near future. I think it's important to test your server in an agent environment, and mcp-use provides that.Matt Wang@mcpjamsMCP-Use is the open source way to connect any LLM to any MCP server and build custom agents that have tool access, without using closed source or application clients.Adam Silverman@adamsilvermanthat's why we love open-source! @NASA is building an agent with MCP using our library @mcpuse 🚀pederzh@pederzhBeen thinking about trying your @mcpuse today when I started building a ChatGPT app, but wasn't sure if it's the right use case. Now I assume it is 🙂Paolo Perazzo@SiVolaGreat addition to the generic MCP Client collection.Kingsley Uyi Idehen@kidehenInfra isn't just plumbing. It's distribution. Examples: AgentMail: inboxes for agents, mcp-use: MCP infra, DeepAware: RL for data center ops. Whoever owns the rails agents use to talk to the outside world ends up with Twilio-style power.Vedika Jain@vedikaja_inmcp-use is by far the best Python framework for building an agent MCP. It's so easy to set up an agent connected to MCP servers through the command line, that way I can quickly iterate on my MCP servers and test. Most people are using MCP servers in chat clients like Cursor / Claude Code, but MCP server use by agents will explode in the near future. I think it's important to test your server in an agent environment, and mcp-use provides that.Matt Wang@mcpjamsMCP-Use is the open source way to connect any LLM to any MCP server and build custom agents that have tool access, without using closed source or application clients.Adam Silverman@adamsilvermanthat's why we love open-source! @NASA is building an agent with MCP using our library @mcpuse 🚀pederzh@pederzhReally sharp execution, mcp-use nails the 'Vercel for MCP' play. Love how you've stripped deployment and aggregation down to a single endpoint with zero friction. Crypto infra teams, onchain data providers, and AI-powered DeFi dashboards could slot this in to speed up agent builds massively.Ctran.eth@ctranbtw🦜🤖 MCP-Use Tools - Just launched: An open-source library that connects any LLM to MCP tools for custom agents, featuring seamless integration with LangChain and support for web browsing, Airbnb search, and 3D modeling capabilities.LangChain@LangChainAIWe implemented Code Mode in mcp-use's MCPClient . All you need to do is define which servers you want your agent to use, enable code mode, and you're done! The client will expose two tools: - One that allows the agent to progressively discover which servers and tools are available - One that allows the agent to execute code in an environment where the MCP servers are available as Python modules (SDKs)Pietro@pietrozulloReally sharp execution, mcp-use nails the 'Vercel for MCP' play. Love how you've stripped deployment and aggregation down to a single endpoint with zero friction. Crypto infra teams, onchain data providers, and AI-powered DeFi dashboards could slot this in to speed up agent builds massively.Ctran.eth@ctranbtw🦜🤖 MCP-Use Tools - Just launched: An open-source library that connects any LLM to MCP tools for custom agents, featuring seamless integration with LangChain and support for web browsing, Airbnb search, and 3D modeling capabilities.LangChain@LangChainAIWe implemented Code Mode in mcp-use's MCPClient . All you need to do is define which servers you want your agent to use, enable code mode, and you're done! The client will expose two tools: - One that allows the agent to progressively discover which servers and tools are available - One that allows the agent to execute code in an environment where the MCP servers are available as Python modules (SDKs)Pietro@pietrozulloBuilt in the open.One of the most adopted open-source MCP frameworks. Open from day one.10k+GitHub stars. comments.author: zxela
comments.textRawHtml:
On your website under "Thousands of dev-teams are building with mcp-use" you include a quote from your CTO (@enri). If thousands of teams are using the product, I would think you could omit this post.

I think you have a really neat product here, but these types of testimonials do you more harm than good; they sour my opinion of all other testimonials on your site. I shouldn't have to play detective.
comments.textZhHtml:
在你们网站的“数千个开发团队正在使用 mcp-use 进行构建”板块下，你们引用了 CTO (@enri) 的一句话。既然有成千上万的团队在使用该产品，我觉得大可不必放这条帖子。

我觉得你们的产品真的很棒，但这类推荐语对你们弊大于利；它们让我对网站上其他所有推荐语都产生了反感。我不该还得像个侦探一样去甄别。

children.author: pzullo
children.textRawHtml: noted! thanks for the feedback, will be gone soon
children.textZhHtml: 已记录！感谢反馈，很快就会修复

comments.author: Brainspackle
comments.textRawHtml: Having to sign up to browse available MCPs stopped me dead in my tracks and made me close the tab.  I won't be the only one.  Perhaps consider allowing people to browse more of the site before you try to force a signup on them?
comments.textZhHtml: 必须注册才能浏览可用的 MCP，这直接让我停下了脚步并关闭了标签页。我不会是唯一一个这样做的人。也许可以考虑在强制用户注册之前，先允许他们多浏览一下网站的其他内容？

children.author: pzullo
children.textRawHtml: hey thanks for the feedback, we do not really offer a "set" of pre made MCPs that we could show pre signup, unfortunately our platform requires you to deploy something to use it - we try to give an overview of all the features in our "Platform" section in the navbar on top
children.textZhHtml: 嘿，感谢反馈，我们并没有提供一套预制的MCP集合供注册前展示，遗憾的是，我们的平台需要您先部署一些东西才能使用——我们尽量在顶部导航栏的“平台”部分提供所有功能的概览

comments.author: msencenb
comments.textRawHtml:
I am really impressed with your demo video, particularly the analytics, logs, and test suite features.

I'm a target customer where I have a few curious customers, but I'm not fully ready to roll it out yet across the customer base. One thing that's stopping me, what does credits mean on your pricing page? And what is the pay as you go price after you hit your limit? I would need to be able to budget this before I deploy.

The second piece - we already have a CLI, which is great for terminal based agents and what we will continue to recommend. What we really want, and I think is what you are offering, is basically an easier way to deploy a 'remote connector' to use Claude lingo so that normal users with the claude/chatgpt app can just use our MCP. Can you point me to guidelines or the right place in your open source templates to understand how I would best handle auth (or the tradeoffs in each) during the initial build phase of the MCP server?
comments.textZhHtml:
你们的演示视频让我印象深刻，尤其是分析、日志和测试套件功能。

我是你们的目标客户，手头也有几个对此感兴趣的客户，但我还没完全准备好在整个客户群中推广。有一个问题阻碍了我，你们定价页面上的“credits”是什么意思？达到额度上限后，按量付费的价格是多少？在部署之前，我需要能够对此进行预算。

第二点——我们已经有了一个 CLI，它非常适合基于终端的代理，我们也会继续推荐它。我们真正想要的，我想也是你们提供的，基本上是一种更简便的方式来部署“远程连接器”，用 Claude 的术语来说，这样使用 claude/chatgpt 应用的普通用户就能直接使用我们的 MCP。能否给我指点一下相关指南，或者在你们的开源模板中指出正确的位置，让我了解在 MCP 服务器的初始构建阶段如何最好地处理身份验证（或各自的权衡）？

children.author: pzullo
children.textRawHtml:
thank you so much we have put a lot of love into our product!

pricing: we offer several credit based products that have different cost - requests, build minutes, eval runs, checklist all have different unit cost the breakdown is here https://docs.manufact.com/dashboard/billing thanks for pointing out that it should be more transparent!

auth: we published a few blogs https://manufact.com/blog/oauth-mcp https://manufact.com/blog/authentication

+ lots of auth templates you can start from https://manufact.com/templates
children.textZhHtml:
非常感谢，我们在产品上倾注了大量心血！

定价：我们提供多种基于积分的产品，成本各不相同——请求、构建分钟数、评估运行、检查清单都有不同的单位成本，明细请见此处 https://docs.manufact.com/dashboard/billing 感谢您指出这里需要更加透明！

身份验证：我们发布了几篇博客 https://manufact.com/blog/oauth-mcphttps://manufact.com/blog/authentication

+ 还有大量身份验证模板供您起步使用 https://manufact.com/templates

comments.author: rgbrgb
comments.textRawHtml: This is cool. I was skeptical of MCP's until I made one recently. They're essentially the exact same as 1) giving your agent a CLI tool or REST API and 2) pointing it there in an AGENT.md/CLAUDE.md. Agents are great at using built-for-human CLI tools and IMO they don't need anything purpose-built for agents. The key difference, which ends up being a usability win for non-technical users, is that the MCP bundles 1 and 2 - harnesses inject the MCP tool descriptions on every session after install. Of course, that's also why you need to be careful about context bloat when using/building them
comments.textZhHtml: 这很酷。直到最近我自己做了一个，才对 MCP 消除了疑虑。它们本质上与以下操作完全相同：1) 给你的代理一个 CLI 工具或 REST API，2) 在 AGENT.md/CLAUDE.md 中指向它。代理非常擅长使用为人类构建的 CLI 工具，在我看来，它们不需要任何专为代理构建的东西。关键的区别在于，这对非技术用户来说是一个可用性上的胜利，MCP 将 1 和 2 打包在一起——安装后，harnesses 会在每次会话中注入 MCP 工具描述。当然，这也是为什么在使用/构建它们时需要注意上下文膨胀的问题

children.author: pzullo
children.textRawHtml: +1 !! Thanks for putting it so clearly, also CLIs and REST have their space in some applications, I think MCP is a way to organize them and distribute them better
children.textZhHtml: +1！！感谢您表达得如此清晰，另外CLI和REST在某些应用中也有其用武之地，我认为MCP是一种更好地组织和分发它们的方式

children.author: nijave
children.textRawHtml:
Yeah MCPs are easier to distribute.

In my experience, they also work better with dumber models than CLIs (which saves money)
children.textZhHtml:
是的，MCP更容易分发。

根据我的经验，与CLI相比，它们在能力较弱的模型上也能更好地工作（这能省钱）

comments.author: Getchowned
comments.textRawHtml: Very interesting, will keep you in mind.
comments.textZhHtml: 非常有趣，我会记住你们的。

comments.author: pacman1337
comments.textRawHtml: stopped using mcp and mostly using skills now. can't understand what this product does or how it could help me.
comments.textZhHtml: 已经停用 MCP，现在主要使用 skills。搞不懂这个产品是做什么的，也不知道它能怎么帮到我。

comments.author: elliotgarreffa
comments.textRawHtml: Love it guys! Also working deep in the MCP space we’ve used manufact right since the start. Great product, team and new release! Congrats
comments.textZhHtml: 太棒了各位！我们也深耕 MCP 领域，从一开始就在用 manufact。产品、团队和全新发布都太棒了！恭喜

comments.author: mmarcelline
comments.textZhHtml: 看起来很棒，恭喜 Pietro & Luigi！

comments.author: ayushrodrigues
comments.textRawHtml: we have so many problems with MCP related to auth, scopes etc... how do you guys help solve that?
comments.textZhHtml: 我们在使用MCP时遇到了很多与身份验证、作用域等相关的问题……你们是如何帮助解决这些问题的？

children.author: tonxxd
children.textRawHtml: One issue i saw related to scopes is the offline_access one that causes frequent reauthenticate request from the client. for example codex has this bug (https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/20503). many servers solve this with some workarounds or increasing the token life. In v2 of mcp-use coming end of the month there should be a builtin way to deal with buggy clients so the server remains connected.
children.textZhHtml: 我注意到的一个与作用域相关的问题是 offline_access，它会导致客户端频繁发起重新身份验证请求。例如 codex 就有这个 bug（https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/20503）。许多服务器通过一些变通方法或延长令牌生命周期来解决这个问题。在本月底即将推出的 mcp-use v2 中，应该会内置一种处理有缺陷客户端的方法，从而保持服务器连接。

children.author: harijoe
children.textRawHtml: You may want to have a look at Skybridge, a TypeScript framework designed to build MCP servers and MCP Apps, with a recent emphasis on making authentication easy.
children.textZhHtml: 您可以了解一下 Skybridge，这是一个专为构建 MCP 服务器和 MCP 应用而设计的 TypeScript 框架，最近的重点是让身份验证变得简单。

children.author: pzullo
children.textRawHtml: we support deploying Skybridge too https://manufact.com/blog/mcp-app-with-skybridge ! saw that you work for the company that is developing that, usually on HN to keep it honest you want to disclose that
children.textZhHtml: 我们也支持部署 Skybridge https://manufact.com/blog/mcp-app-with-skybridge！看到您就职于开发该框架的公司，通常在 HN 上为了保持坦诚，您需要披露这一点

children.author: dotancohen
children.textRawHtml:
TypeScript, for all its benefits, still feels like a toy or project language. I'd love to see a Rust, C++, or even a Go library for such purpose.

I'd love for people with experience to break me of this negativity towards TypeScript. Anybody?
children.textZhHtml:
TypeScript 尽管有诸多优点，但感觉仍然像是一种玩具或项目级语言。我很希望能看到用于此目的的 Rust、C++ 甚至 Go 库。

我希望有经验的人能打消我对 TypeScript 的这种负面看法。有人愿意吗？
